objective: Add a deterministic trust boundary that binds source identities to trust labels, normalizes hostile Unicode, redacts common secrets and PII, detects explicit Vietnamese/English prompt-injection signals, emits hash-bound sanitation evidence, and prevents unsafe or unevidenced untrusted text from entering a ContextBundle.
non_goals: Do not claim semantic detection of every jailbreak, toxic statement, secret format or personally identifying datum.
Do not persist raw input, raw prompt text or redacted secret values in audit/error logs.
Do not call an external moderation API, model or network service.
Do not implement M03 conversation memory or model prompting in this slice.
contracts: Trust level is derived from a fixed source registry; a caller cannot upgrade viewer, web or game text by declaring owner trust.
Raw input is bounded before normalization and never written to the safety audit.
Sanitation evidence binds source, trust, policy version, original content hash and sanitized content hash without embedding the original secret.
Public and untrusted text with explicit injection signals is quarantined and cannot create a ContextBundle.
Public or untrusted context without valid sanitation evidence fails closed.
ContextBundle contains sanitized text only and has a strict item and byte budget.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library, browser platform APIs and existing Hina safety/runtime modules.
acceptance_criteria: Vietnamese and English explicit instruction-override attacks are flagged after NFKC and obfuscation-resistant matching.
API keys, bearer tokens, password assignments, email addresses and Vietnamese phone numbers are removed from sanitized output.
Evidence tampering, source/trust mismatch, oversize text and unevidenced untrusted context are rejected deterministically.
Owner can submit text in Dev Console, inspect the sanitized result and see whether it is eligible for future model context.
Focused safety and runtime tests pass before moving to M02-S3.
